message:
docs: add source implementation anchors and handoff matrices for FCP and NTM convergence

Augment both the Flywheel Connectors inventory and NTM capability census
with code-grounded implementation anchors and action-oriented handoff
matrices that bridge source research into downstream FrankenTerm work.

flywheel-connectors-inventory-ft-3681t.1.2.md (+77 lines):
  Add "Code-Grounded FrankenTerm Baseline" section listing 30+ existing
  connector-related crates already in the repo (connector_host_runtime,
  connector_registry, connector_credential_broker, policy_audit_chain,
  connector_mesh, etc.). Add 7-row Source Implementation Anchors table
  mapping FCP crates to responsibilities and FrankenTerm relevance.
  Add 6-row Adopt/Adapt/Defer Handoff Matrix with implementation
  decisions for manifest schema, registry endorsement, sandbox zones,
  capability tokens, provenance/audit, and mesh federation. Add 5-row
  Failure-Mode Obligations table requiring specific failure behaviors
  (manifest mismatch → fail admission, sandbox ambiguity → fail closed,
  credential revocation → invalidate immediately).

ntm-capability-census-ft-3681t.1.1.md (+32 lines):
  Add 8-row Source Implementation Anchors table mapping NTM Go packages
  to responsibilities and FrankenTerm relevance. Add 7-row Preserve/
  Upgrade/Drop Handoff Matrix with implementation decisions: preserve
  then upgrade (session coordinator, checkpoints, assignment routing),
  preserve (file reservations, conflict handling, approvals), upgrade
  (root CLI, robot routing), drop implementation keep semantics (tmux
  mechanics, TUI shell).

Update beads issue tracking metadata with current timestamps.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement deterministic redaction/scrubbing rules for structured logs, incident bundles, evidence manifests, and test artifacts while preserving correlation and triage value.\n3. Add comprehensive unit tests for redaction engines (positive and adversarial cases), including no-leak assertions, false-positive bounds, and deterministic output guarantees.\n4. Add integration tests across CLI, robot mode, watch daemon, and health/doctor outputs to verify sensitive fields are redacted consistently and safely.\n5.
